text: Modern scientific experiments demand the simultaneous coordination and monitoring of increasingly diverse and complex data streams. However, the inherently serial execution of traditional programming instructions often struggles to capture the asynchronous, parallel nature of real-time experimental data. To address this challenge, we introduce Bonsai: a modular, high-performance, open-source visual programming framework designed for the seamless acquisition and online processing of heterogeneous data. We detail the core architectural principles of the software and demonstrate its utility in the rapid prototyping of integrated experimental designs within neuroscience. Through various use cases, we highlight Bonsai’s capacity to synchronize disparate hardware and software components, including real-time behavioral video tracking, large-scale electrophysiology, and sophisticated closed-loop stimulation protocols.
